schema_note: Pathway data for OT registration + migration skills assessment in Australia. Two parallel processes: (1) AHPRA registration to PRACTICE; (2) OTC migration skills assessment to lodge EOI. As of 27 October 2025, OTC stopped doing registration qualification assessments — those moved to AHPRA. OTC continues to do migration skills assessments as the DHA-appointed assessing authority.

key_dates.2025-10-27: OTBA launches 3 new streamlined pathways. OTC stops accepting Stage 1 applications for registration. AHPRA takes over qualification assessment for registration.
key_dates.2025-09: OTBA publishes full details + lists of recognised qualifications and comparable regulators

registration_pathways.applies_to: International OT graduates from 27 Oct 2025 onwards

step_2.name: Apply to AHPRA for qualification assessment
step_2.note: Fee not publicly published; check AHPRA portal for current rate

step_3_outcomes.outcome_a_recognised: Straight to General Registration — no supervised practice required
step_3_outcomes.outcome_b_relevant_not_equivalent: Practical Assessment of Competence required → supervised practice
step_3_outcomes.outcome_c_not_relevant: Cannot register; consider re-qualifying in Australia

step_4_general_registration.name: Apply for General Registration with AHPRA

registration_pathways.total_timeline_weeks: 12-26 (3-6 months) for recognised outcome; longer for relevant+supervised

registration_pathways.applies_to: OTC applications submitted BEFORE 27 Oct 2025 — still being processed

step_1_otc_stage1.name: OTC Stage 1: Qualification assessment
step_1_otc_stage1.duration: Several months (current delay noted on OTC website)

step_2_limited_registration.name: Apply for Limited Registration (form ALPS-96) via AHPRA portal

step_3_supervised_practice.name: 6 months supervised practice under approved supervisor
step_3_supervised_practice.note: Midway report at 2-3 months; final report and COCA at end

step_4_general_registration.name: Apply for General Registration using COCA

migration_skills_assessment.purpose: Required for EOI lodgement under ANZSCO 252411 (subclass 189/190/491)
migration_skills_assessment.owner: OTC (appointed by Department of Home Affairs)
migration_skills_assessment.scope: Desktop assessment of qualifications + English proficiency
migration_skills_assessment.applies_to: Both Australian-qualified international students AND international graduates
migration_skills_assessment.duration: Several months (current processing delays noted; user should apply 6 months before EOI intended date)

english_requirements.note: OTC + AHPRA may have different English requirements; verify at application time

core: Qualification assessment (per pathway A or B above)
English language proficiency (PTE 65 each / IELTS 7.0 each)
general_registration_requirements.fees_note: AHPRA fees published annually at https://www.ahpra.gov.au/Registration/Registration-Fees; 2025-26 rates apply

post_graduation_timeline.scenario: AHPRA-accredited Australian OT graduate applies post-graduation. Two parallel processes: (1) AHPRA qualification assessment → General Registration; (2) OTC migration skills assessment → EOI.

steps.action: Submit AHPRA qualification assessment (Pathway A) + OTC migration skills assessment in parallel
AHPRA assessment outcome — likely 'substantially equivalent' for an AHPRA-accredited Australian OT program (Tier 1)
Apply for General Registration with AHPRA
Receive OTC migration skills assessment positive outcome
Lodge EOI in SkillSelect with AHPRA General Registration + OTC skills assessment + 491 regional eligibility (if applicable)
Wait for invitation round (quarterly from 2026+). Verify current OT 189 minimum score at lodgement time (the threshold shifts each program year).

post_graduation_timeline.earliest_invite_estimate: Within 4-8 months of graduation (assuming eligibility thresholds met at lodgement)

what_actually_happens_for_cdu_graduate: Pathway A outcome 'substantially equivalent' (most likely for CDU Master) → General Registration direct, no supervised practice required
Processing time: weeks to months per AHPRA ('up to 6 weeks' for initial review, then 4-5 weeks for full assessment per current public statements)
AHPRA student registration: CDU auto-registers all OT students with AHPRA under National Law — no separate student-side action needed

dec_2025_consultation_update.title: Draft registration standard: General registration for experienced internationally qualified health practitioners
dec_2025_consultation_update.applies_to: OT (occupational therapy) is in the consultation list along with dental / medical radiation / podiatry
dec_2025_consultation_update.three_new_pathways: Allow experience-based registration for practitioners from comparable health systems
dec_2025_consultation_update.impact_for_cdu_grad: Not directly relevant (CDU grad is Australian-trained, not internationally qualified) but signals regulatory trend toward more flexible pathways

fees_summary_aud.AHPRA registration (annual): Not publicly listed (verify at portal; ~$300-500 typical)

data_sources.name: AHPRA Occupational Therapy Board — Competence Assessment

key_caveats: 27 Oct 2025 was a major change — pre-2025 timelines no longer apply for new applicants
AHPRA fees are reviewed annually — current 2025-26 rates; verify before application
OTC skills assessment processing times have known delays (mentioned on OTC site)
PTE/IELTS test centres may have wait times — book 2-3 months in advance
AHPRA qualification assessment outcome is NOT transferable to OTC migration assessment — they are separate processes with separate fees
Supervised practice requires finding an Australian employer willing to sponsor limited registration — can be a bottleneck
